content_of_post_to_comment: A surprising number of people don’t argue with what you said. They argue with what they decided you meant. Then they spend three paragraphs debating a point nobody made. At that point, you’re not having a conversation. You’re just watching someone shadowbox their own imagination.
comment: The tricky part is that sometimes people aren't misreading you at all—they're responding to the version of your argument that logically follows from what you said. The line between projection and implication is thinner than we like to admit.
